MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-10-2009 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 612

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES IS GRANTED AS PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS THAT PREVIOUSLY HAD BEEN PROVIDED TO DEFENDANT BONNE BRIDGES AS ALL PRIVILEGES HAVE BEEN WAIVED AS TO THOSE DOCUMENTS. THE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT BY THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE ARE COMMUNICATIONS GIVEN TO DEFENDANT RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE OF BREACH BY BONNE BRIDGES OF A DUTY ARISING OUT OF THE LAWYER-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP. THE PRIVILEGE HAS NOT BEEN WAIVED AS TO ANY DOCUMENTS NOT PROVIDED TO BONNE BRIDGES BY ANYONE DURING THE PERIOD OF THEIR REPRESENTATION OF PLAINTIFF. CONSENT TO THE DISCLOSURE TO BONNE BRIDGES OF WORK PRODUCT AND ATTORNEY CLIENT COMMUNICATIONS IS MANIFESTED BY THE CONDUCT OF THE HOLDERS OF THOSE PRIVILEGES IN PRODUCING THE DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTION BY THOSE HOLDERS OF THE PRIVILEGES. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses;
Off Calendar Jul-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses is granted as Plaintiff is ordered to produce all documents that previously had been provided to Defendant Bonne Bridges as all privileges have been waived as to those documents. The documents sought by the Requests For Production and withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege are communications given to Defendant relevant to the issue of breach by Bonne Bridges of a duty arising out of the lawyer-client relationship. The privilege has not been waived as to any documents not provided to Bonne Bridges by anyone during the period of their representation of Plaintiff. Consent to the disclosure to Bonne Bridges of work product and attorney client communications is manifested by the conduct of the holders of those privileges in producing the documents without objection by those holders of the privileges. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-20-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCE STATED.

Plaintiff David Gilliam, M.D.'s Motion For Protective Order is denied. The court finds that the Request For Admissions propounded by Defendants are warranted by the quantity of existing and potential issues in this case.
Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Leak K. Bolea are overruled as to (a). Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Leak K. Bolea are sustained as to (b) (i)-(vii).
Plaintiff shall respond to Defendants' Request For Admissions within 20 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
